sci2.headline: No spacecraft has ever landed in the outer solar system — except one: the Huygens probe, which parachuted through Titan's orange haze in 2005 and touched down more than a billion kilometres from Earth in cold that dropped below minus 170 degrees Cels - Space Daily

body: Two decades on, the European Huygens probe is still the only spacecraft ever to land in the outer solar system, after parachuting onto Saturn's moon Titan in 2005. For all the spacecraft humanity has sent into the outer solar system, only one has ever landed there.
Everything else, from the Pioneers and Voyagers to Galileo, Cassini and Juno, has flown past or o…

sci3.headline: America's only landing on Venus was an accident — in 1978 NASA dropped four cone-shaped probes into the Venusian atmosphere, none built to survive hitting the ground, and one of them struck the surface, kept transmitting for 67 minutes and 37 seconds, - 19FortyFive
sci3.deck: The United States never built a Venus lander — every American probe was meant to die on impact. But in December 1978 one throwaway probe hit the surface and kept transmitting for 67 minutes and 37 seconds, making America's only Venus landing an accident.
sci3.teaser: The United States never built a Venus lander — every American probe was meant to die on impact. But in December 1978 one throwaway probe hit the surface and kept transmitting for 6…
body: The United States never built a Venus lander — every American probe was meant to die on impact. But in December 1978 one throwaway probe hit the surface and kept transmitting for 67 minutes and 37 seconds, making America's only Venus landing an accident.

The Soviet Union spent the 1960s and 1970s building Venus landers, titanium tanks engineered to survive the solar system’s most hostile surface. The United States never built one at all. Every Americ…
